name: launch-mvp
title: Launch MVP
description: Ships a minimal viable product with defined scope, core build, deployment, and feedback loop. Goal-driven: creates milestones and issues automatically.

goal.title: Launch MVP
goal.description: Ship a minimal viable product. Define scope tightly, build the core feature, deploy it, and iterate based on feedback. The goal is a working product in users' hands, not a polished one.

milestones.title: Define scope
milestones.description: Agree on what the MVP includes and — critically — what it does not.
milestones.completionCriteria: A written scope document exists with explicit in/out-of-scope lists, approved by the team.

milestones.title: Build core feature
milestones.description: Implement the single most important feature that delivers value to users.
milestones.completionCriteria: Core feature works end-to-end in a local development environment.

milestones.title: Deploy
milestones.description: Get the MVP running in a production-like environment accessible to users.
milestones.completionCriteria: Application is deployed, accessible via URL, and basic health checks pass.

milestones.title: Iterate
milestones.description: Collect feedback and ship improvements. Fix critical bugs, improve UX, add missing essentials.
milestones.completionCriteria: At least one round of user feedback has been incorporated and deployed.

issues.title: Create project repository and initial structure
issues.description: Initialize the repository with README, .gitignore, package.json (or equivalent), and a basic project structure. Push to main.

issues.title: Write MVP scope document
issues.description: Draft a scope document that defines what the MVP includes and excludes. List the core user journey, key features (max 3), and explicit non-goals. Get team sign-off.

issues.title: Set up CI pipeline
issues.description: Add a CI workflow (GitHub Actions or equivalent) that runs lint and tests on every push. Keep it simple — single job, no matrix builds.

issues.title: Implement core feature
issues.description: Build the primary feature defined in the scope document. Focus on functionality over polish. Write tests for critical paths.

issues.title: Deploy to production
issues.description: Set up the production environment and deploy the application. Configure DNS, TLS, and basic monitoring/alerting. Verify the deployment with a smoke test.

issues.title: Write basic user documentation
issues.description: Create a getting-started guide that covers the core user journey. Keep it short — one page maximum. Include screenshots or examples where helpful.

issues.title: Collect and triage initial feedback
issues.description: Gather feedback from early users. Categorize into: critical bugs, UX issues, and feature requests. Prioritize critical bugs for immediate fix.

issues.title: Fix critical bugs from feedback
issues.description: Address critical bugs identified during the feedback round. Each fix should include a regression test.
